topic: A person learns their apartment building is going condo
raw_output:
[story 1]

The mail arrived this morning, and it wasn't just junk flyers. It was glossy brochures, crisp and vibrant, detailing the imminent transformation of the old brick building into luxury condominiums. Sarah stared at the glossy pages, her thumb tracing the architectural renderings of the new lobby. A slow, wide smile spread across her face, stretching her cheeks. She bounced on the balls of her feet, a light, almost giddy energy buzzing beneath her skin. "This is it," she whispered to the empty living room, her voice thick with excitement. She immediately pulled out her phone, not to check the time, but to start scrolling through potential floor plans, her fingers flying across the screen with unrestrained enthusiasm. The prospect of new marble floors and panoramic views made her feel light, as if she could float.

[story 2]

Mark reread the notice taped to the elevator bank for the third time. The words—'Condominium Conversion Underway'—seemed to shimmer in his vision. He let out a sharp, involuntary laugh, a sound that startled the quiet hallway. He walked back to his apartment, running a hand over the cool plaster wall. His heart was thumping a rapid rhythm against his ribs, a frantic, joyful beat. He started pacing the length of his small space, his movements quick and jerky. "Can you believe it?" he exclaimed to the air, his tone bright and high. He couldn't stop grinning; the sheer possibility of moving into a space with a balcony made his entire body feel buoyant, ready to spring into action.

[story 3]

When the developer’s presentation finally landed on the community board, David didn't just listen; he absorbed every detail, his eyes shining with an almost alarming brightness. He leaned back in his chair, a slow, deep breath escaping him. When the meeting adjourned, he didn't gather his papers immediately. Instead, he stood up, stretching his arms high above his head, a broad, open expression on his face. "This changes everything," he announced to the few neighbors lingering near the exit, his voice ringing with pure, uncontained delight. He felt a surge of energy, a powerful need to start planning, to envision the future space waiting for him, his mind racing ahead with dazzling possibilities.
